title: Farsetia somalensis
intro: Farsetia somalensis is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Brassicaceae, commonly found in dry regions of East Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, which include treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Farsetia somalensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often made into a herbal tea or a tincture, as well as being used in the form of infusions and decoctions, to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Farsetia somalensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. This plant is native to the Horn of Africa.
